items.title: Apple shelves Vision Pro revamp in pursuit of AI glasses to rival Meta - Bloomberg
items.description: Investing.com -- Apple Inc (NASDAQ:AAPL) is shelving development of a lower-cost Vision Pro headset to prioritize a more urgent initiative: building smart glasses that could rival offerings from Meta Platforms Inc (NASDAQ:META). The company is reallocating internal resources in a bid to gain ground in a growing segment of AI-powered wearables that many in the tech industry view as the successor to the smartphone, according to Bloomberg, citing sources familiar with the matter.

items.title: Apple pulled off a quiet rebound, but here’s the lever many missed
items.description: As analysts fixate on what Apple lacks in AI, something far more subtle is quietly driving its stock higher.

items.title: Apple halts Vision Pro overhaul to focus on AI glasses, Bloomberg News reports
items.description: The iPhone maker had been preparing a cheaper, lighter variant of its headset, code-named N100, for release in 2027, Bloomberg said.  The $3,499 Vision Pro, launched in February 2024 to heavy fanfare, has struggled to sustain momentum amid a thin slate of mainstream content and competition from cheaper devices such as Meta's Quest.  Apple is working on at least two types of smart glasses, the report said.

items.title: OpenAI Is Breaking New Ground With Sora. Why the AI Video App Is Bad News for Meta.
items.description: Sora is now the No. 3 most downloaded app on Apple’s App Store. It’s launching a new wave of user-generated AI videos.
